# fix(w3): security re-review fix-immediate — payload bounds + discriminator

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `ff63504ddc6e` — fix(w3): security re-review fix-immediate — payload bounds + discriminator

**Body**:

Closes the four W3-introduced findings security-reviewer surfaced on
HEAD b3faa4d0 (HIGH workflow_blob size cap, MEDIUM task_id validation,
MEDIUM soft_row_cap doc + constructor, LOW discriminator dispatch).
Same-bug-class as the prior 66e76106 hardening commit; per
rules/autonomous-execution.md Rule 4 the gap fits within the shard
budget so the fix lands inline rather than in a follow-up PR.

Why these matter on the queue path:

- workflow_blob unbounded -> multi-MB JSON OOMs every dequeueing worker
  on json.loads. The cap is the structural defense at the producer
  boundary; SQLTaskQueue.enqueue mirrors it as defense-in-depth.
- task_id unvalidated -> caller-supplied PK could shadow the W3
  stable-hash dedup namespace; charset + length cap closes the
  poisoning vector.
- queue row-count uncapped -> table fills disk over deployment lifetime
  without operator action; soft_row_cap WARN (rate-limited) gives
  operators an actionable signal without dropping work.
- hasattr duck-type on workflow.to_dict -> Rule 3d sibling pattern
  (silent fallback when the to_dict accessor flips to a non-callable
  attribute on a future subclass); isinstance discriminator with
  class-level to_dict fallback covers stubs without re-introducing
  silent flip.

Carried-forward findings (TOCTOU on SQLite chmod + missing WAL/SHM
chmod) are pre-existing on main and are NOT same-bug-class as W3's
dispatch contract; filed as a separate follow-up issue.
